tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Four Teenagers Rush Into Burning Home, Save 89-Year-Old and His Memories
stories.summary: Four 19-year-old friends spotted a house fire at midnight and instantly sprang into action, rescuing an elderly man and his most treasured possessions from the flames.

stories.headline: Strangers Rebuild Navy Vet's Storm-Damaged Kitchen So He Can Feed Hungry Neighbors
stories.summary: Navy Chef Lamont Harris received an outpouring of love when strangers rebuilt his outdoor kitchen destroyed by storms, restoring his mission to feed Chester's homeless neighbors.

stories.headline: Tiny Cape Verde Defies History, Becomes Smallest Nation to Reach World Cup Knockout Stage
stories.summary: Current summary unchanged - article excerpt contains insufficient substantive content about Cape Verde's World Cup achievement.

stories.headline: Vietnam Brings Spotted Deer Back From 40 Years of Extinction in the Wild
stories.summary: Cuc Phuong National Park is bringing Sika deer—spotted animals with elegant white markings—back to Vietnam's wild after nearly 40 years of extinction.

stories.headline: Beekeepers Drop Everything to Rescue 20 Million Bees From Overturned Truck
stories.summary: Texas beekeepers abandoned their Sunday to rush to an accident scene and work together rescuing millions of bees from an overturned truck.

stories.headline: Flamingos Return to South Punjab Wetlands After Nearly 40 Years Gone
stories.summary: Eight flamingos returned peacefully to South Punjab's Rakh Khanpur Sanctuary in May, marking nearly 40 years since the species last graced the wetlands.

stories.headline: Serena Williams Returns to Wimbledon Singles at 44, Embracing One More Chance at Centre Court
stories.summary: At 44, Serena Williams accepted Wimbledon's wild card to face 20-year-old Maya Joint on Centre Court, seizing what she calls "this could be it."

stories.headline: Canada Scores First-Ever World Cup Knockout Win With 92nd-Minute Heart-Stopper
stories.summary: Stephen Eustáquio's dramatic stoppage-time goal gave Canada a 1-0 victory over South Africa, marking a historic first knockout stage win for the nation.

stories.headline: Cornwall Wins Award for Planting Rare Rainforest Back Into Being
stories.summary: Cornwall Wildlife Trust planted 4,600 native trees to resurrect West Looe's temperate rainforest, winning a national award from 320 nominees.

stories.headline: India Opens World's First Nuclear-Powered Hydrogen Plant, Blazing a Clean Energy Path
stories.summary: Current summary unchanged - article excerpt contains insufficient substantive content about India's nuclear-powered hydrogen plant.
